message:
perf(core): renewal-amortization statement micro-batcher for small-N ceremony

AAC-P6: add an opportunistic micro-batcher that coalesces consecutive
identical prepared-statement invocations inside a transaction into a
single ceremony epoch. Each `execute_with_params` call still returns
row-by-row; the coalescing is invisible to callers.

Ceremony pieces amortized across an epoch (precompiled-DML fast lane,
in-txn):
- per-call schema_cookie / schema_generation / function_registry_generation
  comparison (the body of `ensure_schema_unchanged_with_prebound_publication`
  for :memory: paths)
- per-call `PreparedDmlEntryProof` construction

Flush triggers (epoch renewal): different prepared-statement program
pointer (Arc identity), different parameter arity, schema drift
(cookie/generation/func-registry bump), txn boundary
(BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK/SAVEPOINT/RELEASE), error on the previous call,
row-budget exhaustion (default 16), or wall-clock deadline (default
200 µs) sampled lazily near the budget ceiling.

PRAGMAs (all connection-scoped, SAFE defaults):
- `fsqlite.stmt_microbatch`        ON
- `fsqlite.stmt_microbatch_max_r`  16
- `fsqlite.stmt_microbatch_max_us` 200
- `fsqlite.stmt_microbatch_stats`  observability (hits / renewals)

Benchmark — `INSERTThroughput — Single Transaction — tiny_1col`, in
same process with micro-batcher flipped ON/OFF, 4 warmup + 60 iters,
1-column `INTEGER PRIMARY KEY` single-txn loop:

    rows   OFF med   r=16 med   r=64 med   r=256 med   hits/run (r=16)
     100    248.1    243.9      248.6      248.0          93
    1000   1963.2   1983.1     1984.0     1952.8         937
   10000  18906.1  19150.1    19154.4    18946.7        9374

Honest interpretation: hit rate is ~93-99% on every loop, confirming
the mechanism is active, but the observable throughput delta is
within ±2% measurement noise. The existing `bd-pktso` in-txn fast
path already skips almost every amortizable piece, so what remains
to coalesce (schema cookie / function-registry cell reads) is near
the noise floor for the `:memory:` single-txn scenarios targeted by
Gap #5. The mechanism is now in place; future ceremony additions
will amortize along this hook instead of paying per-call.

Correctness: when the carry hits, identity equality over
`(program_ptr, binds_arity, schema_cookie, schema_generation,
function_registry_generation, in_explicit_txn, concurrent_session_id)`
is a superset of the checks performed by
`ensure_schema_unchanged_with_prebound_publication` for in-txn
:memory: paths, so skipping that call preserves its invariants. The
carry is only ever entered from the precompiled-DML fast lane with
`active_txn.is_some() && in_transaction.get()` and never for
statements that may observe connection-scoped change tracking.

Tests: 5 new unit tests cover PRAGMA defaults, ON/OFF semantics,
hit counters across consecutive inserts, flush-on-COMMIT, and the
disabled-no-carry invariant. Full `cargo test -p fsqlite-core
--release --lib` reports 2893 passed / 75 failed / 4 ignored — the
same numbers as on the baseline (b4143ae3), so no new regressions.

Benchmark binary added at
`crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/bin/microbatch_bench.rs` so future tuning of
`max_r` / `max_us` can be re-measured in the same process with the
only changing variable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
